description_long: This object is a historical-style cannon mounted on a wooden wheeled carriage. The cannon barrel is long, cylindrical, and made of dark metal, featuring a bulbous rear section and a slightly flared muzzle. The carriage is constructed from thick wooden planks, reinforced with metal brackets and bolts, and is equipped with four solid wooden wheels for mobility. Metal rings and hooks are attached to the sides of the carriage, likely for maneuvering or securing the cannon. The overall appearance is robust and utilitarian, with visible wear and a matte finish on both wood and metal surfaces. The design suggests it was intended for use in siege or defensive scenarios, rather than modern urban combat.
description: A historical cannon with a long metal barrel mounted on a sturdy wooden wheeled carriage, featuring metal reinforcements and maneuvering rings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the cannon barrel, the wooden carriage, and two wheels with visible metal rings and hooks.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular muzzle of the cannon and the alignment of all four wheels beneath the carriage.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the cannon barrel, carriage, and wheels, mirroring the details of view 0.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the breech of the cannon and the back of the wooden carriage, with all four wheels visible.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed cannon barrel mounted centrally on the wooden carriage.
